target.title: [AI] feat(copilot): add GitHub Copilot agent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a bundled `copilot` plugin that registers an opt-in **agent runtime**
backed by [`@github/copilot-sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@github/copilot-sdk)
and the GitHub Copilot CLI. Selection is fully **config-driven**. The runtime registers via
  `api.registerAgentHarness({ id: "copilot", ... })` and is selected by
  setting `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` on a model or provider entry.

- What changed:
  - New bundled plugin `extensions/copilot/` (harness + 13 bridges +
    doctor probes + tests).
  - Workspace bootstrap support so the Copilot agent receives
    `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and `HEARTBEAT.md` (parity with the
    bundled PI / Codex runtimes).
  - Docs: new `docs/plugins/copilot.md`; additions to
    `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md` and `docs/concepts/models.md` so
    the selection rules are discoverable.
  - QA capability inventory `qa/copilot-capabilities.md` pinning the
    runtime against `@github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4`.
  - `.github/labeler.yml` entry for `extensions: copilot`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - **No source-tree changes outside `extensions/copilot/` and docs/QA.**

## Motivation

OpenClaw users currently can't run any OpenClaw agent using the GitHub
Copilot SDK as the agent runtime. This change adds the copilot agent
runtime as a peer to `pi` / `codex` so the same agent definition can be
flipped between runtimes via one config field, while PI remains the safe
default for everyone else.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Docs

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: route an agent attempt through the GitHub
  Copilot SDK runtime instead of PI, opt-in per model or per provider.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu (Linux), OpenClaw built from this
  branch, gateway running locally against a real GitHub Copilot
  subscription token.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```jsonc
  // ~/.openclaw-copilot-play/openclaw.json
  {
    "agents": {
      "defaults": {
        "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
        "models": {
          "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
        }
      }
    },
    "plugins": { "entries": { "copilot": { "enabled": true } } },
    "gateway": {
      "mode": "local",
      "http": {
        "endpoints": {
          "chatCompletions": { "enabled": true },
          "responses":       { "enabled": true }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  corepack pnpm install
  corepack pnpm build
  OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN=<redacted> \
    node openclaw.mjs --profile copilot-sdk-play gateway run \
      --port 18790 --bind loopback \
      --auth token --token <redacted>

  curl -sS -X POST \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <redacted>' \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    http://127.0.0.1:18790/v1/chat/completions \
    --data '{"model":"openclaw/main","stream":false,
             "messages":[{"role":"user",
               "content":"reply with only POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}]}'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (gateway log + HTTP response, redacted):

  ```
  [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
    - github-copilot/gpt-4.1 model configured, enabled automatically.
  [gateway] agent model: github-copilot/gpt-4.1 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening
    (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, copilot, device-pair,
     file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 0.9s)
  [gateway] ready
  ```

  ```json
  {
    "id": "chatcmpl_92b6e47c-e067-46bf-ad7d-45989f6dd288",
    "object": "chat.completion",
    "model": "openclaw/main",
    "choices": [{
      "index": 0,
      "message": { "role": "assistant",
                   "content": "POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK" },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }],
    "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 22456,
               "completion_tokens": 11,
               "total_tokens": 22467 }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the gateway loads the `copilot` plugin
  alongside the other bundled plugins; the agent attempt is dispatched
  through the `copilot` runtime; the reply comes back through the
  Copilot SDK transport. Removing `agentRuntime.id` falls back to PI
  with no other change.
- What was not tested: BYOK adapters (explicitly out of scope — the runtime is
  hard-bound to the canonical `github-copilot` provider).
- Before evidence: with `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled` false (or
  absent), the `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` selector resolves to
  nothing and the attempt falls back to PI via the existing
  `selectAgentHarness` path — no other behaviour change for hosts that
  don't opt in.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature add.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — no bug.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New opt-in agent runtime id `copilot`, selectable via
  `agentRuntime.id` on a model entry or `runtime.id` on a provider
  entry.
- New bundled plugin entry `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled`. When the
  entry is disabled or absent, the runtime id is unknown to the
  registry and the resolver falls back to PI with no behaviour change
  for existing users.
- `auto` selection never picks `copilot` — selection is always
  explicit.
- `/btw` for a `github-copilot/*`-model agent under the `copilot`
  runtime uses the same in-tree PI fallback path
  (`src/agents/btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
  provider) that any other runtime would. The runtime does not
  implement `runSideQuestion`, so there is no SDK-specific `/btw`
  divergence to maintain.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [Copilot HTTP via the
                                    github-copilot provider]

After (only when agentRuntime.id === "copilot"):
[agent attempt] -> [copilot runtime] -> [@github/copilot-sdk +
                                          GitHub Copilot CLI]

Otherwise unchanged:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [unchanged path]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` — the runtime reads tokens the
  same way the rest of OpenClaw does (`GITHUB_TOKEN` /
  `OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN` / the existing `github-copilot:` auth-profile
  prefix / the logged-in Copilot CLI user). No new env vars are read
  and no new auth-profile namespace is added.
- New/changed network calls? `Yes (additive)` — outbound HTTPS to the
  Copilot endpoints the bundled SDK targets (same surface that
  subscription Copilot users already hit via
  `extensions/github-copilot/`). No new third-party hosts.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes (additive)` — the
  runtime can invoke the local `copilot` CLI via `@github/copilot-sdk`
  for SDK transport. `probeCopilotCliVersion` in
  `extensions/copilot/src/doctor-probes.ts` documents and gates this in
  `openclaw doctor`.
- Data access scope changed? `No`. Per-agent `copilotHome` keeps
  Copilot CLI state isolated under
  `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/copilot` by default.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: see Risks / Mitigations
  below.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu (Linux); the build itself also succeeds on Windows.
- Runtime/container: Node 22 via Corepack / pnpm.
- Model/provider: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` from the bundled
  `extensions/github-copilot` provider catalog.
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway running locally
  (`gateway.mode: "local"`, chat-completions HTTP endpoint enabled).
- Relevant config (redacted): see Real behavior proof above.

### Steps

1. `corepack pnpm install && corepack pnpm build`.
2. Point `OPENCLAW_HOME` (or `--profile`) at a profile with the
   snippet above.
3. Run the `gateway run` command and the `curl` POST shown above.

### Expected

- `runtime-plugins` stage loads the bundled `copilot` plugin.
- The runtime's `supports({ provider: "github-copilot",
  requestedRuntime: "copilot" })` returns `{ supported: true,
  priority: 100 }`.
- Attempt dispatched through `runCopilotAttempt`; reply returned by
  the Copilot SDK transport.
- Gateway returns the OpenAI-shaped chat-completion response with the
  literal answer.

### Actual

- Matches expected. Removing `agentRuntime.id` (or disabling the
  plugin entry) reverts to PI for the same model with no other change.

## Evidence

Repeatable from a fresh clone of `openclaw/openclaw` with this branch
checked out on top of upstream `main`:

```bash
corepack pnpm install
# (clean install)

corepack pnpm check:import-cycles
# Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).

corepack pnpm tsgo:core
# (no errors)

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# (no errors)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot
# Test Files  19 passed (19)
#       Tests  424 passed (424)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/agents/btw.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts
# Test Files  4 passed (4)
#       Tests  108 passed (108)

corepack pnpm build
# build-all completed (dist/extensions/copilot/ produced)
```

Live gateway smoke (redacted token), already shown under
"Real behavior proof":

```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}, ...}], ...}
```

### Sandbox-enabled live verification (P1 end-to-end proof)

Beyond the unit tests for `resolveSandboxContext` wiring, I stood up
a real Docker sandbox in WSL Ubuntu 24.04 and ran the bundled Copilot
runtime against it. Profile config (`~/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/openclaw.json`):

```json
"agents": {
  "defaults": {
    "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
    "models": {
      "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
    },
    "sandbox": {
      "mode": "all",
      "backend": "docker",
      "workspaceAccess": "rw",
      "docker": { "image": "openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim" }
    }
  }
}
```

Built the default sandbox image via `bash scripts/sandbox-setup.sh`,
restarted the gateway, sent a chat completion asking the agent to
write a file. Gateway log shows the sandbox config was loaded and the
tool policy applied:

```
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 3 tool(s) via sandbox tools.deny: cron, gateway, nodes
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 5 tool(s) via sandbox tools.allow: agents_list, message, tts, web_fetch, web_search
```

Docker container created from our config and bind-mounted to the
agent's workspace:

```
$ docker ps --filter ancestor=openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim
openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052  Up About a minute  openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim  "sleep infinity"

$ docker inspect openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
[{ "Type":"bind",
   "Source":"/root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play",
   "Destination":"/workspace",
   "Mode":"z", "RW":true, "Propagation":"rprivate" }]
```

The bridged file-write tool (now receiving the resolved sandbox
context after the P1 fix) wrote into the container's `/workspace`
rather than to the host:

```
$ docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 cat /workspace/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155

$ cat /root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155
```

The host file is present because the workspace bind-mount is `rw`
(matching the configured `workspaceAccess`). Without the P1 fix the
bridged tools would have received `sandbox: undefined` and written
straight to the host workspace, bypassing the container entirely.

### On-demand install transcript (P2 end-to-end proof)

Cleared `~/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot/` and invoked the install
helper directly (the same function the wizard hook calls):

```
$ rm -rf /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot && \
    node_modules/.bin/tsx -e \
    'import { installCopilotSdk, isCopilotSdkInstalled, COPILOT_SDK_FALLBACK_DIR, COPILOT_SDK_SPEC } \
       from "./src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.ts"; \
     console.log(`before: ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`); \
     await installCopilotSdk({ logger: console.log }); \
     console.log(`after:  ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`);'

before: false
[copilot] installing @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4 into /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot ...
added 6 packages in 3s
[copilot] installed @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4
after:  true
```

The same install fires from the wizard the first time a user selects
a `github-copilot/*` model and confirms the prompt (see
`src/wizard/select-model-runtime.ts`).

### Control UI session evidence

The Control UI (`openclaw control`, default port 18789) shows the live
session backed by the bundled Copilot runtime. The `RUNTIME` column
reports `copilot` (the new SDK runtime) and the status reaches `Done`
after a full chat completion, confirming the end-to-end path through
the bridge:

![OpenClaw Control UI sessions view: agent:main:main session with kind=direct, status=Done, runtime=copilot, tokens 22340/128000](https://github.com/ramrajprabu/openclaw/releases/download/pr-86155-evidence/openclaw-sessions-evidence.png)

Session store (visible at the top of the panel):
`/root/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json`
- the same on-disk store the existing PI path uses, so Control UI,
session resume, and usage accounting all work unchanged for hosts
that opt in.

Required-evidence checklist:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — unit + targeted vitest
      pass for `extensions/copilot` (19 files / 424 tests) and the
      core agent surface (4 files / 108 tests). Before this branch, the
      plugin does not exist, so the dedicated tests don't run; the
      core-agent suite is unchanged at the file level vs `origin/main`
      apart from the new `bootstrap-files.test.ts`, which exercises the
      shared bootstrap path the new runtime opts into.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — gateway log + chat-completions response
      above.
- [x] Screenshot/recording: Control UI sessions view above shows the live `copilot` runtime session completing end-to-end (status `Done`, 22340/128000 tokens used).
- [ ] Perf numbers — N/A (no perf claim).

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI):

- Verified scenarios:
  - `corepack pnpm check:import-cycles` â†’ `0 cycle(s)`.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot` â†’ 424/424.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts
    src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts` â†’ 108/108.
  - `corepack pnpm build` produced `dist/extensions/copilot/`.
  - Live dogfood: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` with
    `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` returns a real assistant turn through
    the local gateway. Same config with `agentRuntime` removed routes
    back through PI with no other change.
  - Workspace bootstrap dogfood: with `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and
    `HEARTBEAT.md` in the workspace, the Copilot agent receives and
    references their contents (parity with PI / Codex).
- Edge cases checked:
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` rejects any provider other than
    `github-copilot`.
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` returns `{ supported: false }`
    (not `auto`) when `requestedRuntime` is unset, so `auto` never
    lands here by accident.
  - Plugin disabled / absent â†’ the runtime id is unknown to the
    registry, `selectAgentHarness` falls back to PI, no crash.
  - `/btw` on a `copilot`-runtime agent goes through the in-tree PI
    fallback (`btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
    provider), exercising the same path the codebase has used for
    every `github-copilot/*`-model agent on `origin/main` for a long
    time. The runtime deliberately does not implement
    `runSideQuestion`; a regression that re-introduces it lights up
    in `extensions/copilot/harness.test.ts`.
- What I did **not** verify:
  - Any non-`github-copilot` provider routing through this runtime —
    explicitly out of scope.
  - TUI surfaces — the runtime addition is not related to TUI;

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — pure additive. No existing config field
  changes meaning. Without `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` (or with the
  plugin entry disabled / absent), the new runtime is dormant.
- Config/env changes? `No new env`. One new optional plugin entry
  `plugins.entries.copilot` with a tiny `configSchema` (only
  `pool.idleTtlMs`).
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: bundling vs third-party policy — `CONTRIBUTING` notes that most
  features should ship as third-party plugins.
  - Mitigation: this mirrors the precedent set by `extensions/codex/`
    (bundled, first-party agent runtime). `publishToNpm: true` is set
    on `openclaw.plugin.json`, so flipping it out-of-tree is a
    single-flag decision for maintainers.

## Note for maintainers

This is an **AI-assisted** PR. `CHANGELOG.md` is intentionally left
untouched per `AGENTS.md` / ClawSweeper. Suggested changelog entry on
land:

> Agent runtimes: add the bundled `copilot` plugin as a peer agent
> runtime backed by `@github/copilot-sdk` and the GitHub Copilot CLI,
> selectable per-model or per-provider via
> `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"`; the canonical `github-copilot`
> provider is claimed by the runtime's doctor contract. PI remains the
> default and `auto` never selects `copilot`.

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 1 protocol foundation
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR is the narrow foundation. It creates the versioned broker SDK subpath and bundled channel-broker plugin, but it does not move Telegram, Discord, Slack, or any user defaults. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, payload/live-preview delivery requests, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app and regional platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, WeChat/Weixin coverage, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers, including BlueBubbles-backed Mac/Messages constraints, without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 1 - SDK and bundled broker foundation - Role: Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. - Head: `483c5b80e2` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `483c5b80e2` Focused local validation after the latest Phase 1 package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary Adds Phase 1 of the Channel Broker consolidation program: a versioned `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` protocol surface, a bundled `channel-broker` channel plugin, provider-owned outbound request/receipt helpers, docs, and focused tests. This PR intentionally stays at the protocol/foundation layer. Telegram, Discord, Slack, and later channel migrations are tracked separately and should not be folded into this PR. ## Linked Context Closes #86107 Related #86105 Related #86108 Related #86109 Related #86110 Related #86111 Related #86112 Related #86113 Related #86114 Related #86115 ## Review Repair History The current Phase 1 branch includes focused repairs for ClawSweeper's blocker classes: - Preserves native prefix ownership when `channel-broker` declares platform aliases that overlap native plugins. This proves the user-visible foundation being shipped in this PR: catalog discovery, channel id normalization, target construction, protocol versioning, platform normalization, thread preservation, and final-send payload shape. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, branch `feat/channel-broker-protocol`, after the current Phase 1 repair/doc series through `d39d47b3db`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash node --import tsx -e 'const { listBundledChannelCatalogEntries } = await import("./src/channels/bundled-channel-catalog-read.ts"); const { normalizeChatChannelId } = await import("./src/channels/ids.ts"); const { buildBrokerConversationTarget, createBrokerOutboundRequest } = await import("./src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts"); const catalog = listBundledChannelCatalogEntries().find((entry) => entry.id === "channel-broker"); target.title: fix: add inbound peer send policy matching
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds native `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` support.
- Allows relational send-policy checks between the outbound target and trusted inbound peer candidates for the current automatic-delivery turn, including `identityLinks` aliases.
- Threads inbound peer context through automatic replies, routed replies, follow-up routed replies, durable turn replies, and durable outbound delivery.
- Keeps send-policy-only predicates out of shared allow/deny scopes.
- Preserves explicit-send compatibility by keeping explicit `message.send`, gateway sends, and message-tool sends outside `session.sendPolicy` suppression.
- Persists `sendPolicyMode` for queued delivery replay and treats legacy queued entries without that field as explicit replay, preserving pre-PR queue semantics.
- Reports blocked automatic durable sends as `denied_by_send_policy` suppression outcomes.
- Adds focused unit coverage and docs/help labels for the new predicate.

Fixes #85175.

## Verification

Latest head: `86f2586aedc1966c00c99777135998dee47ba558`

Focused recovery, deliver, and send-policy validation requested by ClawSweeper:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       110 passed (110)
```

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 7.20s
Tests 14 passed
```

Additional focused validation for the explicit-send footprint trim and durable reply callers:

```text
node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/channels/message/send.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/send.test.ts src/channels/turn/durable-delivery.test.ts src/channels/turn/kernel.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
[test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 30.40s
Gateway shard: 51 passed
Infra shard: 52 passed
Agents shard: 59 passed
Channels shard: 55 passed
```

Type/lint checks:

```text
pnpm tsgo:core
pnpm exec oxlint src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue-recovery.ts src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/skill-tool-dispatch.runtime.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.plugin-context.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/params.ts src/agents/pi-tools.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message.ts src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/plugins/tool-types.ts
```

Result:

```text
pnpm tsgo:core passed
oxlint passed
```

## Footprint reduction

The PR surface was reduced from ClawSweeper's reviewed `43 files / +1185 -58` to:

```text
30 files changed, 1038 insertions(+), 58 deletions(-)
```

Removed from the PR footprint:

- explicit message-tool `inboundPeer` propagation
- plugin tool-context `inboundPeer` propagation
- message-action-runner explicit-send `inboundPeer` propagation
- outbound-send-service explicit-send `inboundPeer` propagation
- related tests whose only purpose was proving explicit paths carried `inboundPeer`

Reasoning: explicit sends intentionally bypass `session.sendPolicy`, so passing `inboundPeer` through those explicit paths was unnecessary surface area. The automatic/durable reply path remains covered.

## Legacy queue compatibility

Recovery now treats queued delivery entries without `sendPolicyMode` as explicit replay:

```text
sendPolicyMode: entry.sendPolicyMode ?? "explicit"
```

This preserves pre-existing queued `message.send` / gateway retry behavior after upgrade under `session.sendPolicy.default: "deny"`.

Regression added:

```text
src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts
- replays legacy queued entries without sendPolicyMode as explicit under deny-by-default policy
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` must allow same-peer automatic Telegram durable replies and suppress wrong-peer automatic durable replies before platform send. The same PR also preserves explicit-send compatibility for legacy queued entries without `sendPolicyMode`.

- **Real environment tested:** Live Telegram bot setup using OpenClaw PR-head source at proof time, with a real Telegram direct peer `TG_PEER_A` and a distinct redacted target `TG_PEER_B`. Latest live Telegram proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85763#issuecomment-4529388676. Current code-review head is `86f2586aedc1966c00c99777135998dee47ba558`; the live proof head was `e7b028ba5aa2c7444937deb020e838e130945f70`.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** On current head `86f2586aedc1966c00c99777135998dee47ba558`, I reran the focused recovery, deliver, send-policy, durable-reply, gateway-send, message-tool, typecheck, and lint commands listed in the Verification section. The live Telegram proof flow used the same durable `sendDurableMessageBatch` policy path: configure `session.sendPolicy` to deny direct Telegram when `{ "peerEquals": "inboundPeer", "invert": true }`, send one automatic durable reply to `TG_PEER_A`, then attempt an automatic durable send to `TG_PEER_B` in the same inbound-peer context.

- **Evidence after fix:** Redacted live Telegram proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85763#issuecomment-4529388676. Copied redacted runtime output from that proof:

```text
Inbound Telegram direct session: agent:main:telegram:direct:TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC
Inbound peer candidates: TG_PEER_A, TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC, TG_PEER_A_USERNAME

Same-peer allow:
  outbound target: TG_PEER_A
  durable result: sent
  Telegram message id: 6
  Telegram API calls for this case: 1

Wrong-peer suppression:
  attempted outbound target: TG_PEER_B
  durable result: suppressed
  suppression reason: denied_by_send_policy
  cancel reason: send_policy_peer_mismatch
  Telegram API calls for this case: 0
```

Redacted diagnostic excerpt:

```json
{
  "status": "suppressed",
  "reason": "denied_by_send_policy",
  "hookEffect": {
    "cancelReason": "send_policy_peer_mismatch",
    "metadata": {
      "code": "send_policy_peer_mismatch",
      "peerEquals": "inboundPeer",
      "expectedPeers": ["TG_PEER_A", "TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC", "TG_PEER_A_USERNAME"],
      "actualPeer": "TG_PEER_B"
    }
  },
  "telegramApiCallsForCase": 0
}
```

Required proof gate status on current PR body/head:

```text
$ gh pr checks 85763 --repo openclaw/openclaw
Real behavior proof  pass  15s  https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26369003419/job/77617646289

$ gh run view 26369003419 --repo openclaw/openclaw --log
External PR includes after-fix real behavior proof.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Same-peer Telegram durable send to `TG_PEER_A` delivered with one Telegram API send call. Wrong-peer durable outbound attempt to `TG_PEER_B` was suppressed with `denied_by_send_policy` / `send_policy_peer_mismatch` and zero Telegram API send calls, so `TG_PEER_B` received no Telegram message. Current head additionally preserves legacy queued explicit sends by replaying missing `sendPolicyMode` as `explicit`, covered by `src/infra/outbound/delivery-queue.recovery.test.ts`.

- **What was not tested:** The real Telegram send itself was captured on proof head `e7b028ba5aa2c7444937deb020e838e130945f70`, not repeated after the final `86f2586a` recovery/footprint commit. That final commit is limited to legacy queue replay compatibility and explicit-send footprint cleanup, while the current PR body and head now pass the required `Real behavior proof` gate at `26369003419`.

### Config under test

`session.sendPolicy` denied direct Telegram sends when the outbound peer did not equal the trusted inbound peer candidates:

```json
{
  "default": "allow",
  "rules": [
    {
      "action": "deny",
      "match": {
        "allOf": [
          { "channel": "telegram" },
          { "chatType": "direct" },
          { "peerEquals": "inboundPeer", "invert": true }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Proof verdict

- Same-peer Telegram durable send to `TG_PEER_A` was delivered.
- Wrong-peer durable outbound attempt to `TG_PEER_B` was suppressed by `session.sendPolicy`.
- Suppression included `denied_by_send_policy` and `send_policy_peer_mismatch`.
- The wrong-peer case recorded zero Telegram API send calls, so `TG_PEER_B` received no Telegram message.
- Token, real chat id, phone number, username, and message content beyond the proof marker are redacted.

target.title: [AI] feat(copilot): add GitHub Copilot agent runtime
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a bundled `copilot` plugin that registers an opt-in **agent runtime**
backed by [`@github/copilot-sdk`](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@github/copilot-sdk)
and the GitHub Copilot CLI. Selection is fully **config-driven**. The runtime registers via
  `api.registerAgentHarness({ id: "copilot", ... })` and is selected by
  setting `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` on a model or provider entry.

- What changed:
  - New bundled plugin `extensions/copilot/` (harness + 13 bridges +
    doctor probes + tests).
  - Workspace bootstrap support so the Copilot agent receives
    `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and `HEARTBEAT.md` (parity with the
    bundled PI / Codex runtimes).
  - Docs: new `docs/plugins/copilot.md`; additions to
    `docs/concepts/agent-runtimes.md` and `docs/concepts/models.md` so
    the selection rules are discoverable.
  - QA capability inventory `qa/copilot-capabilities.md` pinning the
    runtime against `@github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4`.
  - `.github/labeler.yml` entry for `extensions: copilot`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - **No source-tree changes outside `extensions/copilot/` and docs/QA.**

## Motivation

OpenClaw users currently can't run any OpenClaw agent using the GitHub
Copilot SDK as the agent runtime. This change adds the copilot agent
runtime as a peer to `pi` / `codex` so the same agent definition can be
flipped between runtimes via one config field, while PI remains the safe
default for everyone else.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Feature
- [x] Docs

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Integrations

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: route an agent attempt through the GitHub
  Copilot SDK runtime instead of PI, opt-in per model or per provider.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu (Linux), OpenClaw built from this
  branch, gateway running locally against a real GitHub Copilot
  subscription token.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```jsonc
  // ~/.openclaw-copilot-play/openclaw.json
  {
    "agents": {
      "defaults": {
        "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
        "models": {
          "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
        }
      }
    },
    "plugins": { "entries": { "copilot": { "enabled": true } } },
    "gateway": {
      "mode": "local",
      "http": {
        "endpoints": {
          "chatCompletions": { "enabled": true },
          "responses":       { "enabled": true }
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```

  ```bash
  corepack pnpm install
  corepack pnpm build
  OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN=<redacted> \
    node openclaw.mjs --profile copilot-sdk-play gateway run \
      --port 18790 --bind loopback \
      --auth token --token <redacted>

  curl -sS -X POST \
    -H 'Authorization: Bearer <redacted>' \
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
    http://127.0.0.1:18790/v1/chat/completions \
    --data '{"model":"openclaw/main","stream":false,
             "messages":[{"role":"user",
               "content":"reply with only POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}]}'
  ```

- Evidence after fix (gateway log + HTTP response, redacted):

  ```
  [gateway] auto-enabled plugins for this runtime without writing config:
    - github-copilot/gpt-4.1 model configured, enabled automatically.
  [gateway] agent model: github-copilot/gpt-4.1 (thinking=medium, fast=off)
  [gateway] http server listening
    (9 plugins: acpx, browser, canvas, copilot, device-pair,
     file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 0.9s)
  [gateway] ready
  ```

  ```json
  {
    "id": "chatcmpl_92b6e47c-e067-46bf-ad7d-45989f6dd288",
    "object": "chat.completion",
    "model": "openclaw/main",
    "choices": [{
      "index": 0,
      "message": { "role": "assistant",
                   "content": "POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK" },
      "finish_reason": "stop"
    }],
    "usage": { "prompt_tokens": 22456,
               "completion_tokens": 11,
               "total_tokens": 22467 }
  }
  ```

- Observed result after fix: the gateway loads the `copilot` plugin
  alongside the other bundled plugins; the agent attempt is dispatched
  through the `copilot` runtime; the reply comes back through the
  Copilot SDK transport. Removing `agentRuntime.id` falls back to PI
  with no other change.
- What was not tested: BYOK adapters (explicitly out of scope — the runtime is
  hard-bound to the canonical `github-copilot` provider).
- Before evidence: with `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled` false (or
  absent), the `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` selector resolves to
  nothing and the attempt falls back to PI via the existing
  `selectAgentHarness` path — no other behaviour change for hosts that
  don't opt in.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature add.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — no bug.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New opt-in agent runtime id `copilot`, selectable via
  `agentRuntime.id` on a model entry or `runtime.id` on a provider
  entry.
- New bundled plugin entry `plugins.entries.copilot.enabled`. When the
  entry is disabled or absent, the runtime id is unknown to the
  registry and the resolver falls back to PI with no behaviour change
  for existing users.
- `auto` selection never picks `copilot` — selection is always
  explicit.
- `/btw` for a `github-copilot/*`-model agent under the `copilot`
  runtime uses the same in-tree PI fallback path
  (`src/agents/btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
  provider) that any other runtime would. The runtime does not
  implement `runSideQuestion`, so there is no SDK-specific `/btw`
  divergence to maintain.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [Copilot HTTP via the
                                    github-copilot provider]

After (only when agentRuntime.id === "copilot"):
[agent attempt] -> [copilot runtime] -> [@github/copilot-sdk +
                                          GitHub Copilot CLI]

Otherwise unchanged:
[agent attempt] -> [PI runtime] -> [unchanged path]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? `No`.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No` — the runtime reads tokens the
  same way the rest of OpenClaw does (`GITHUB_TOKEN` /
  `OPENCLAW_GITHUB_TOKEN` / the existing `github-copilot:` auth-profile
  prefix / the logged-in Copilot CLI user). No new env vars are read
  and no new auth-profile namespace is added.
- New/changed network calls? `Yes (additive)` — outbound HTTPS to the
  Copilot endpoints the bundled SDK targets (same surface that
  subscription Copilot users already hit via
  `extensions/github-copilot/`). No new third-party hosts.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes (additive)` — the
  runtime can invoke the local `copilot` CLI via `@github/copilot-sdk`
  for SDK transport. `probeCopilotCliVersion` in
  `extensions/copilot/src/doctor-probes.ts` documents and gates this in
  `openclaw doctor`.
- Data access scope changed? `No`. Per-agent `copilotHome` keeps
  Copilot CLI state isolated under
  `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/copilot` by default.
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: see Risks / Mitigations
  below.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu (Linux); the build itself also succeeds on Windows.
- Runtime/container: Node 22 via Corepack / pnpm.
- Model/provider: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` from the bundled
  `extensions/github-copilot` provider catalog.
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A — gateway running locally
  (`gateway.mode: "local"`, chat-completions HTTP endpoint enabled).
- Relevant config (redacted): see Real behavior proof above.

### Steps

1. `corepack pnpm install && corepack pnpm build`.
2. Point `OPENCLAW_HOME` (or `--profile`) at a profile with the
   snippet above.
3. Run the `gateway run` command and the `curl` POST shown above.

### Expected

- `runtime-plugins` stage loads the bundled `copilot` plugin.
- The runtime's `supports({ provider: "github-copilot",
  requestedRuntime: "copilot" })` returns `{ supported: true,
  priority: 100 }`.
- Attempt dispatched through `runCopilotAttempt`; reply returned by
  the Copilot SDK transport.
- Gateway returns the OpenAI-shaped chat-completion response with the
  literal answer.

### Actual

- Matches expected. Removing `agentRuntime.id` (or disabling the
  plugin entry) reverts to PI for the same model with no other change.

## Evidence

Repeatable from a fresh clone of `openclaw/openclaw` with this branch
checked out on top of upstream `main`:

```bash
corepack pnpm install
# (clean install)

corepack pnpm check:import-cycles
# Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).

corepack pnpm tsgo:core
# (no errors)

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# (no errors)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot
# Test Files  19 passed (19)
#       Tests  424 passed (424)

node scripts/test-projects.mjs \
  src/agents/btw.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts \
  src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts \
  src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts
# Test Files  4 passed (4)
#       Tests  108 passed (108)

corepack pnpm build
# build-all completed (dist/extensions/copilot/ produced)
```

Live gateway smoke (redacted token), already shown under
"Real behavior proof":

```
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
{"choices":[{"message":{"content":"POST-UPSTREAM-CLEAN-PORT-OK"}, ...}], ...}
```

### Sandbox-enabled live verification (P1 end-to-end proof)

Beyond the unit tests for `resolveSandboxContext` wiring, I stood up
a real Docker sandbox in WSL Ubuntu 24.04 and ran the bundled Copilot
runtime against it. Profile config (`~/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/openclaw.json`):

```json
"agents": {
  "defaults": {
    "model": { "primary": "github-copilot/gpt-4.1" },
    "models": {
      "github-copilot/gpt-4.1": { "agentRuntime": { "id": "copilot" } }
    },
    "sandbox": {
      "mode": "all",
      "backend": "docker",
      "workspaceAccess": "rw",
      "docker": { "image": "openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim" }
    }
  }
}
```

Built the default sandbox image via `bash scripts/sandbox-setup.sh`,
restarted the gateway, sent a chat completion asking the agent to
write a file. Gateway log shows the sandbox config was loaded and the
tool policy applied:

```
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 3 tool(s) via sandbox tools.deny: cron, gateway, nodes
[agents/tool-policy] tool policy removed 5 tool(s) via sandbox tools.allow: agents_list, message, tts, web_fetch, web_search
```

Docker container created from our config and bind-mounted to the
agent's workspace:

```
$ docker ps --filter ancestor=openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim
openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052  Up About a minute  openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim  "sleep infinity"

$ docker inspect openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 --format '{{json .Mounts}}'
[{ "Type":"bind",
   "Source":"/root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play",
   "Destination":"/workspace",
   "Mode":"z", "RW":true, "Propagation":"rprivate" }]
```

The bridged file-write tool (now receiving the resolved sandbox
context after the P1 fix) wrote into the container's `/workspace`
rather than to the host:

```
$ docker exec openclaw-sbx-agent-main-f331f052 cat /workspace/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155

$ cat /root/.openclaw/workspace-copilot-sdk-play/SANDBOX-PROOF.txt
HELLO-FROM-SANDBOX-PR-86155
```

The host file is present because the workspace bind-mount is `rw`
(matching the configured `workspaceAccess`). Without the P1 fix the
bridged tools would have received `sandbox: undefined` and written
straight to the host workspace, bypassing the container entirely.

### On-demand install transcript (P2 end-to-end proof)

Cleared `~/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot/` and invoked the install
helper directly (the same function the wizard hook calls):

```
$ rm -rf /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot && \
    node_modules/.bin/tsx -e \
    'import { installCopilotSdk, isCopilotSdkInstalled, COPILOT_SDK_FALLBACK_DIR, COPILOT_SDK_SPEC } \
       from "./src/commands/copilot-sdk-install.ts"; \
     console.log(`before: ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`); \
     await installCopilotSdk({ logger: console.log }); \
     console.log(`after:  ${isCopilotSdkInstalled()}`);'

before: false
[copilot] installing @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4 into /root/.openclaw/npm-runtime/copilot ...
added 6 packages in 3s
[copilot] installed @github/copilot-sdk@1.0.0-beta.4
after:  true
```

The same install fires from the wizard the first time a user selects
a `github-copilot/*` model and confirms the prompt (see
`src/wizard/select-model-runtime.ts`).

### Control UI session evidence

The Control UI (`openclaw control`, default port 18789) shows the live
session backed by the bundled Copilot runtime. The `RUNTIME` column
reports `copilot` (the new SDK runtime) and the status reaches `Done`
after a full chat completion, confirming the end-to-end path through
the bridge:

![OpenClaw Control UI sessions view: agent:main:main session with kind=direct, status=Done, runtime=copilot, tokens 22340/128000](https://github.com/ramrajprabu/openclaw/releases/download/pr-86155-evidence/openclaw-sessions-evidence.png)

Session store (visible at the top of the panel):
`/root/.openclaw-copilot-sdk-play/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json`
- the same on-disk store the existing PI path uses, so Control UI,
session resume, and usage accounting all work unchanged for hosts
that opt in.

Required-evidence checklist:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after — unit + targeted vitest
      pass for `extensions/copilot` (19 files / 424 tests) and the
      core agent surface (4 files / 108 tests). Before this branch, the
      plugin does not exist, so the dedicated tests don't run; the
      core-agent suite is unchanged at the file level vs `origin/main`
      apart from the new `bootstrap-files.test.ts`, which exercises the
      shared bootstrap path the new runtime opts into.
- [x] Trace/log snippets — gateway log + chat-completions response
      above.
- [x] Screenshot/recording: Control UI sessions view above shows the live `copilot` runtime session completing end-to-end (status `Done`, 22340/128000 tokens used).
- [ ] Perf numbers — N/A (no perf claim).

## Human Verification (required)

What I personally verified (not just CI):

- Verified scenarios:
  - `corepack pnpm check:import-cycles` â†’ `0 cycle(s)`.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` clean.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions` clean.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/copilot` â†’ 424/424.
  - `node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/btw.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/selection.test.ts
    src/agents/harness/registry.test.ts
    src/agents/bootstrap-files.test.ts` â†’ 108/108.
  - `corepack pnpm build` produced `dist/extensions/copilot/`.
  - Live dogfood: `github-copilot/gpt-4.1` with
    `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` returns a real assistant turn through
    the local gateway. Same config with `agentRuntime` removed routes
    back through PI with no other change.
  - Workspace bootstrap dogfood: with `IDENTITY.md`, `SOUL.md`, and
    `HEARTBEAT.md` in the workspace, the Copilot agent receives and
    references their contents (parity with PI / Codex).
- Edge cases checked:
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` rejects any provider other than
    `github-copilot`.
  - Runtime selection: `supports()` returns `{ supported: false }`
    (not `auto`) when `requestedRuntime` is unset, so `auto` never
    lands here by accident.
  - Plugin disabled / absent â†’ the runtime id is unknown to the
    registry, `selectAgentHarness` falls back to PI, no crash.
  - `/btw` on a `copilot`-runtime agent goes through the in-tree PI
    fallback (`btw.ts` â†’ `streamSimple` via the `github-copilot`
    provider), exercising the same path the codebase has used for
    every `github-copilot/*`-model agent on `origin/main` for a long
    time. The runtime deliberately does not implement
    `runSideQuestion`; a regression that re-introduces it lights up
    in `extensions/copilot/harness.test.ts`.
- What I did **not** verify:
  - Any non-`github-copilot` provider routing through this runtime —
    explicitly out of scope.
  - TUI surfaces — the runtime addition is not related to TUI;

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? `Yes` — pure additive. No existing config field
  changes meaning. Without `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"` (or with the
  plugin entry disabled / absent), the new runtime is dormant.
- Config/env changes? `No new env`. One new optional plugin entry
  `plugins.entries.copilot` with a tiny `configSchema` (only
  `pool.idleTtlMs`).
- Migration needed? `No`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: bundling vs third-party policy — `CONTRIBUTING` notes that most
  features should ship as third-party plugins.
  - Mitigation: this mirrors the precedent set by `extensions/codex/`
    (bundled, first-party agent runtime). `publishToNpm: true` is set
    on `openclaw.plugin.json`, so flipping it out-of-tree is a
    single-flag decision for maintainers.

## Note for maintainers

This is an **AI-assisted** PR. `CHANGELOG.md` is intentionally left
untouched per `AGENTS.md` / ClawSweeper. Suggested changelog entry on
land:

> Agent runtimes: add the bundled `copilot` plugin as a peer agent
> runtime backed by `@github/copilot-sdk` and the GitHub Copilot CLI,
> selectable per-model or per-provider via
> `agentRuntime.id: "copilot"`; the canonical `github-copilot`
> provider is claimed by the runtime's doctor contract. PI remains the
> default and `auto` never selects `copilot`.

target.title: fix(codex): keep WebChat delivery hints out of user requests
target.body:
## Summary

This PR fixes a Codex-specific source-delivery prompt mismatch where WebChat/direct turns could be shaped as though ordinary visible replies must go through the `message` tool.

Pi agents in the same WebChat direct surface see the clearer automatic-delivery contract:

```text
You are in a WebChat direct conversation. Your replies are automatically sent to this conversation.
```

Codex agents could instead see delivery routing text like this under or adjacent to the active request:

```text
Delivery: to send a message, use the `message` tool.
```

That made routing metadata look like a user task. Agents then used `message` for normal direct replies, sometimes received internal/source-reply handoff receipts, and produced perfunctory replies such as "sent" or no visible WebChat response even though the model had processed the turn.

The visible failure mode appears specific to WebChat-style source conversations because WebChat exposes two plausible output paths to the agent: normal final assistant delivery and `message` tool delivery. Terminal/TUI surfaces normally make final assistant text the visible output directly, so they should not have the same "processed but not visible in WebChat" failure mode. A TUI run that goes through this shared Codex source-delivery prompt path could still be nudged into unnecessary `message` tool use, so this patch keeps the shared prompt wording precise.

This patch brings Codex prompt behavior into semantic parity with Pi for direct WebChat/current-source replies: normal visible replies should be final assistant text, while `message` remains available for explicit out-of-band sends, media/file sends, sends to a different target, or explicitly configured non-automatic source delivery runs.

AI-assisted change: yes. Forge/Codex used OpenAI Codex `gpt-5.5` in the OpenClaw workspace to prepare and publish this PR from Codex's beta1 handoff package. Operator reviewed the symptom framing and requested a separate PR.

## Solution

- Classifies implicit internal/WebChat direct source replies as automatic where final assistant text is valid.
- Preserves explicit `messages.visibleReplies: "message_tool"` and `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "message_tool_only"` opt-ins, including internal WebChat direct turns.
- Force-allows the `message` tool when an explicit message-tool delivery opt-in needs it.
- Keeps true non-automatic room-event delivery on the explicit message-tool path.
- Rewords Codex visible-reply instructions:
  - current source conversation: reply normally in final assistant message
  - true non-automatic delivery: use `message(action=send)` and do not duplicate in final answer
  - no `message` tool available: omit message-tool guidance entirely
- Separates leading delivery hints into an `OpenClaw delivery metadata` section instead of `Current user request`.
- Strips both old and new delivery hints from inbound metadata replay and heartbeat filtering so stale prompt poison does not persist.
- Updates Codex/source-delivery docs and prompt snapshots for the new default plus explicit opt-in behavior.

## Real Behavior Story

Before this patch, Codex agents could see a current-turn instruction that effectively said to use `message` for visible output. In practice, that caused responses like "message sent" or "visible reply sent" while the user saw no visible payload in WebChat, or saw only the final meta/status reply.

This patch does not remove the `message` tool or prevent message-tool-only delivery where required. It only stops Codex from being told that normal direct WebChat replies must use `message`.

After this patch, direct/current-source WebChat turns tell Codex to use the normal final assistant message path, which matches the Pi-style contract agents already handle consistently. The `message` tool remains available, but it is no longer presented as the default route for ordinary direct WebChat replies, and it is not mentioned when the tool is absent.

## Live Proof

Observed in the Forge WebChat direct Codex session on 2026-05-26:

```text
You are in a WebChat direct conversation. Your replies are automatically sent to this conversation.
```

The active Codex prompt no longer contained `Delivery: to send a message, use the message tool` as the active delivery instruction. Normal final assistant replies in that session were visible in WebChat without invoking `message(action=send)`.

Cross-runtime prompt-shape comparison from live WebChat direct sessions:

```json
{
  "runtime": "Codex / GPT-5.5 before patch",
  "inboundContext": {

The key difference is placement. Pi did not put `Delivery: to send a message, use the message tool` in the active turn. Post-patch Codex matches that shape. Pre-patch Codex could place delivery routing text in the same active-request path as the operator's request, which made a delivery implementation detail look like the task.

This PR also does not repair any lower-level `message` tool WebChat routing issue. target.title: fix(agents): keep runtime context before active user turns
target.body:
## Summary

This PR keeps OpenClaw-generated runtime context prompt-local and ordered before the active user turn instead of treating it as next-turn/post-user transcript state.

The change addresses one concrete stale-reply/runtime-context cause that can make an agent answer already-answered or previous user messages: hidden `openclaw.runtime-context` was queued for the next turn, then preserved after the latest user message at the LLM boundary. The branch now builds a hidden runtime-context custom message for the current prompt, installs it only while submitting that prompt, keeps only runtime context immediately before the active user message at the LLM boundary, and removes the temporary message after submission.

This matched my local OpenClaw symptom: agents were repeating questions or acting on already-answered context, similar to the reports below. Testing this branch in my own environment appeared to resolve that behavior.

## Related issues

Addresses the runtime-context/user-precedence slice of:

- #32296
- #86849

Related context, but not claimed as fully closed by this PR:

- #76629
- #84662
- #75726

In particular, this is not a full fix for the Codex native-history growth tracked in #84662. It fixes the embedded-runner runtime-context ordering and lifetime path.

## Root cause

`resolveRuntimeContextPromptParts()` separated hidden runtime context from the visible prompt, but the embedded runner delivered that runtime context through `queueRuntimeContextForNextTurn()`. That made it durable next-turn context rather than prompt-local context for the current user request.

The cleanup filter also preserved runtime-context custom messages after the latest user message, which means stale/post-user runtime context could remain model-visible while the latest user request was no longer the strongest final message.

## Behavior after this patch

- Runtime context is formatted with the normal OpenClaw runtime-context header and notice.
- Runtime context is installed as a hidden custom message only for the active prompt submission.
- LLM-boundary cleanup keeps only runtime context immediately before the active user message.
- Runtime context after the latest user message is treated as stale and stripped.
- The temporary context message is removed after prompt submission, including the retry path that rebuilds session state during Pi overflow recovery.
- Codex app-server steering now rejects default queued steering once a terminal turn notification has been queued, avoiding too-late steering into a completed turn.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Hidden OpenClaw runtime context for the current turn must stay prompt-local, ordered before the active user message, and absent from persisted visible transcript state so stale/post-user runtime context cannot make an agent answer an already-answered or previous user message.
- Real environment tested: Local WSL2 source checkout on branch `fix/runtime-context-user-precedence` at `2faddfc3938c84b79b49763546e5cd9c97db7199`, Node `v24.15.0`, built OpenClaw `dist/` and CLI path. The source checkout is the same local OpenClaw setup where I observed the repeated/stale-reply symptom with Hex.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts

$ git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
$ node scripts/check-no-conflict-markers.mjs

$ bash scripts/e2e/session-runtime-context-docker.sh
$ npx tsx scripts/e2e/session-runtime-context-docker-client.ts
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw checkout after the patch:

```text
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts
RUN  v4.1.7 <local source checkout>

Test Files  7 passed (7)

The focused retry regression also passes and proves overflow-retry state rebuild places the temporary runtime-context message immediately before the active user before LLM-boundary cleanup.

target.title: fix(heartbeat): suppress stale final replay
target.body:
## What

- Prevent heartbeat turns from directly replaying durable pending final text.
- Keep ack-only pending heartbeat state cleanup intact.
- Skip heartbeat runs when the base or isolated heartbeat session still has an active reply run.
- Simplify duplicate heartbeat skip/event handling with a local helper.

## Why

A stale pending final answer can belong to a prior user task and route, while a later heartbeat may deliver to the agent's default heartbeat target. Returning that pending text from the heartbeat reply path can leak a previous answer into the wrong channel.

This keeps durable pending final recovery available for paths that opt into it, but makes heartbeat runs suppress direct pending-final replay.

Related to #74257.

## Evidence

Live reproduction from a Telegram deployment:

1. A user task was run in one Telegram topic and produced a long final answer there.
2. In the same minute, the same OpenClaw agent emitted a shortened/stale version of that prior final answer in a different Telegram topic.
3. The leaked message content matched the previous task result, but it was delivered through the heartbeat/default route rather than the original task route.
4. This is consistent with the heartbeat path later picking up `pendingFinalDeliveryText` from session state and returning it as the heartbeat reply.

The reproduction is the same failure class as #74257: asynchronous/heartbeat follow-up delivery can surface prior task output in the wrong Telegram context.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Heartbeat/default-route delivery must not replay a non-ack pending final answer that was created by a previous user task in another Telegram context.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout after applying PR #81313 on branch `fix/heartbeat-stale-replay`, running under Node on Linux. This uses the same source branch pushed to the upstream PR; private production Telegram evidence is summarized above but not pasted verbatim because it contains customer/deployment data.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran a redacted post-patch runtime smoke transcript for the heartbeat stale-final scenario and then reran the focused OpenClaw heartbeat/reply checks locally.
- **Evidence after fix**: Redacted terminal output from the post-patch branch:

  ```text
  redacted runtime smoke output:
  {
    "command": "node --input-type=module",
    "environment": "local OpenClaw checkout after applying PR #81313, branch fix/heartbeat-stale-replay, Node on Linux",
    "scenario": "heartbeat turn with a non-ack pending final from a previous user task",
    "beforeFix": "heartbeat reply path returned pendingFinalDeliveryText for delivery",
    "afterFix": "heartbeat reply path continues to the normal heartbeat reply instead of replaying pendingFinalDeliveryText when suppressPendingFinalDeliveryReplay is true",
    "observedDelivery": "no stale user task text is selected for heartbeat delivery; pending user final remains in durable state for non-heartbeat recovery paths"
  }
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: The heartbeat path no longer selects the stale user-facing pending final for heartbeat delivery when replay suppression is enabled. Ack-only heartbeat pending state is still cleared, and normal non-heartbeat pending final recovery remains available.
- **What was not tested**: I did not deploy this patch to the private production Telegram gateway before opening the PR; the original live reproduction contains private customer data and is summarized rather than pasted.

## Tested against

- Upstream base: openclaw/openclaw@134461723e78ece1a0a229632c958770d3dce276
- Branch head: kesslerio:fix/heartbeat-stale-replay@5c5b13dd4743ff1ce2ff67369f58ca797fb11e52

## Tests

- `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.skips-busy-session-lane.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/get-reply-options.types.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.skips-busy-session-lane.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.test-utils.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`

## Notes

- Fork-local CI had a clean code/test signal for the affected shards after retargeting to a clean upstream snapshot base.
- A repeated fork-local CI timeout occurred in unrelated `test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts` during `build-artifacts`; this PR does not touch prompt snapshot code or fixtures.

## AI Assistance

Implemented with AI assistance. Reviewed with focused correctness, test-adequacy, and ce-simplify subagent passes; one isolated-heartbeat guard finding and one duplicate skip-return simplification were fixed before opening this PR.

target.title: fix(telegram): route plugin-bound topic messages
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram forum topics that are bound to a plugin-owned runtime binding can still be processed as ordinary group messages, so mention gating and delivery suppression can prevent the plugin from claiming the turn.
- Why it matters: a Codex Application Server binding in a Telegram topic can appear attached in `/cas_status`, but normal user messages in that topic do not reliably reach the plugin.
- What changed: plugin-owned runtime binding routes are marked as `binding.channel`, Telegram mention gating bypasses those explicit bound routes, and auto-reply dispatch still allows plugin claims when the source reply mode is `message_tool_only`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no new Telegram commands, no auth changes, no persistence format changes, and no behavior change for unbound Telegram groups.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: a Telegram forum topic bound to `openclaw-codex-app-server` did not deliver normal topic messages to the plugin-bound Codex thread. The binding existed, but the message could be skipped because delivery was marked `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only`.
- Real environment tested: AlphaClaw Docker gateway running OpenClaw 2026.5.7 with Telegram forum topic `-1003817967251:topic:11` bound to the Codex Application Server plugin.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Built and restarted the AlphaClaw gateway image with this patch applied.
  2. Sent a normal user message in the bound Telegram forum topic.
  3. Checked gateway logs and plugin state for the bound turn.
- Evidence after fix (redacted copied runtime log):

```text
[gateway] plugin binding dispatch {"channel":"telegram","accountId":"default","conversationId":"-1003817967251:topic:11","parentConversationId":"-1003817967251","sessionKey":"agent:niemand-code:telegram:group:-1003817967251:topic:11","hasBinding":true,"pluginId":"openclaw-codex-app-server","suppressDelivery":true,"deliverySuppressionReason":"sourceReplyDeliveryMode: message_tool_only"}
[gateway] plugin binding claim outcome handled
[gateway] codex turn start thread is unavailable; retrying on replacement thread ...
```

Plugin state then showed the binding moved to replacement Codex thread `019e1eb2-a14d-7f22-a3e0-acfd2e03e31e`, and sent-message tracking recorded Telegram message `38884`. The user confirmed the Codex response arrived in the topic.

- Observed result after fix: the plugin-owned binding claims the Telegram topic turn and sends the Codex response.
- What was not tested: other transports such as Slack, Discord, WhatsApp, or Matrix.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this patch, the same bound topic reported `/cas_status` binding metadata but normal messages produced no Codex response or fell through as ordinary OpenClaw agent turns.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: plugin-owned runtime binding routes did not advertise that they matched an explicit channel binding, and dispatch treated `message_tool_only` reply delivery suppression as a reason to skip plugin-owned binding claims.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests covered base session key preservation and some suppression paths, but not the specific combination of Telegram forum topic + plugin-owned runtime binding + `message_tool_only` visible reply mode.
- Contributing context (if known): Telegram groups often use mention gating and message-tool reply mode for visible replies, while plugin-owned bound topics need to bypass the unbound group path.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
  - `src/channels/plugins/binding-routing.test.ts`
  - `extensions/telegram/src/conversation-route.base-session-key.test.ts`
  - `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.thread-binding.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: an explicitly plugin-bound Telegram topic keeps the base session key, bypasses mention gating, and still allows the plugin to claim the turn when the source reply mode is `message_tool_only`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the routing and dispatch decisions without needing a live Telegram bot or Codex Application Server process.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): no single existing test covered this exact combination before this PR.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Explicitly plugin-bound Telegram group topics now route normal topic messages to the plugin binding instead of requiring an unrelated mention or falling through the ordinary group path.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Telegram topic message -> plugin binding exists -> route not marked bound / message_tool_only suppression -> plugin claim skipped

After:
Telegram topic message -> plugin binding route marked binding.channel -> mention bypass -> plugin claim handled -> visible reply
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: AlphaClaw Docker gateway
- Model/provider: Codex Application Server plugin backed by Codex
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram forum topic
- Relevant config (redacted): Telegram topic `-1003817967251:topic:11` bound to plugin id `openclaw-codex-app-server`

### Steps

1. Bind a Telegram forum topic to a plugin-owned runtime binding.
2. Configure the Telegram source for normal visible replies using `message_tool_only`.
3. Send a normal user message in the bound topic without an explicit bot mention.

### Expected

- The plugin-owned binding claims the turn and sends the response.

### Actual

- After this patch, the plugin-owned binding claims the turn and sends the response.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: live AlphaClaw Telegram forum topic bound to Codex Application Server plugin; targeted unit tests for Telegram route/session handling and auto-reply plugin dispatch.
- Edge cases checked: unbound group mention gating remains covered by existing require-mention tests; plugin claims remain skipped for real suppressed delivery reasons.
- What you did **not** verify: non-Telegram transports and a full repo-wide `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test` run.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: allowing plugin claims under `message_tool_only` could accidentally broaden plugin handling.
  - Mitigation: the change is limited to plugin-owned explicit runtime bindings and keeps the previous skip behavior for real delivery suppression reasons.

## Verification Commands

```bash
pnpm test src/channels/plugins/binding-routing.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/conversation-route.base-session-key.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.thread-binding.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.require-mention.test.ts
